name: sing-box

command: set -eo pipefail; SECRET="$(ct-server-core admin clash-secret)"; curl -sf -m 10 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SECRET" "$SINGBOX_CLASH_URL/version" | jq -r .version

note: GPL-3.0. Actively maintained Go reimplementation of the NaiveProxy server protocol with multi-user basic_auth support, built-in ACME, HTTP/3, and a clash-API for hot config reload. Replaces the unmaintained klzgrad/forwardproxy plugin in this stack. The wire protocol is identical to forwardproxy@naive, so existing naive clients connect unchanged. We bundle sing-box as a separate process (not statically linked) so its GPL-3 stays scoped to its own image; our proprietary code is unaffected. Verify probe is the Cycle 2 / 4 drift-detection upgrade (v0.0.42) — runs the authenticated clash-API GET /version endpoint from inside the panel container (reachable via the ct-clash management network's static IP, alias `ct-singbox-mgmt`). Bearer secret is derived by `ct-server-core admin clash-secret` (single source of truth: same `singbox::clash_secret()` function the renderer uses, so no implementation drift can desync the probe from sing-box's actual configured secret). The `set -eo pipefail` shell discipline closes the silent-failure trap where a failed curl + empty-stdin jq could land at exit 0 in some jq versions. Pin `1.13.11` matches docker/sing-box/Dockerfile::ARG SING_BOX_VERSION — operators on an older deployed image will see VersionMismatch on the panel page until `make update` rebuilds. Probe evolution: v0.0.34 introduced TCP-open + echo (broken at the soft-version-match stage); v0.0.35 silenced it; v0.0.37 declared the silencing honestly via expect_no_version_line: true; v0.0.42 finally restores real drift detection by querying sing-box's own clash-API for its identity. expect_zero_exit: true means missing seed / wrong bearer / 401 / connection-refused / cli-missing / hung daemon all flip the row to VerifyFailed; a deployed sing-box whose /version doesn't contain the pinned string flips to VersionMismatch.
